title: Resyntha
shortDescription: AI-powered research intelligence platform that automates literature review, knowledge extraction, cross-paper analysis, and interactive exploration via a multi-agent pipeline.

overview.problem: Researchers face information overload — manually reviewing hundreds of papers, extracting key findings, and identifying research gaps is slow, inconsistent, and does not scale.
overview.solution: Resyntha orchestrates multiple AI agents across a stage-based asynchronous pipeline to automate literature reviews. It searches academic sources via multi-provider retrieval, deduplicates and validates papers, extracts structured knowledge, builds research landscapes, detects gaps, and provides an AI copilot for interactive exploration over generated artifacts.

details.architecture: Production-oriented FastAPI backend with a stage-based pipeline engine driving asynchronous execution via ARQ and Redis. SQLAlchemy manages PostgreSQL persistence for versioned artifacts. A React + TypeScript frontend with TanStack Query orchestrates real-time pipeline state. Multi-provider retrieval supports Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, and arXiv. Groq accelerates LLM inference for extraction and analysis stages. The copilot retrieves exclusively over investigation artifacts using hybrid semantic + keyword search with pgvector.
technicalChallenges: Designing a stage-based pipeline engine with deterministic validation between stages
Managing asynchronous task execution with Redis queue and result backpressure
Building versioned artifact storage supporting rollback and diff
Orchestrating multi-agent coordination without deadlock or duplicated work
Implementing caching strategies across API sources to respect rate limits
Supporting multi-provider retrieval with normalized result formats
Building an investigation-aware copilot that never uses external knowledge
details.learnings: Deepened understanding of pipeline architecture design, asynchronous task orchestration, multi-agent coordination patterns, building observability into distributed data workflows, production-oriented backend architecture, and the complexities of grounding AI responses exclusively in retrieved artifacts.

futureImprovements: Interactive research landscape visualization with graph exploration
Custom agent creation interface for domain-specific extraction
Collaborative review sessions with shared workspaces
Automated literature alerting and monitoring for new publications
Expanded source adapters for PubMed, IEEE, and Scopus
